tree.setup: You are defending your PhD dissertation in computational linguistics at Peking University. Your research proposes a novel framework for analyzing code-switching patterns between Mandarin and English in Chinese social media. The defense committee includes three senior professors, one of whom — a renowned sociolinguist — has expressed skepticism about your theoretical framework's ability to account for the social motivations behind code-switching, arguing that your computational approach reduces complex sociolinguistic behavior to mere pattern matching.
tree.setup_zh: 你正在北京大学进行计算语言学博士论文答辩。你的研究提出了一个分析中国社交媒体上中英语码转换模式的新框架。答辩委员会由三位资深教授组成，其中一位著名社会语言学家对你的理论框架提出了质疑，认为你的计算方法无法解释语码转换背后的社会动机，将复杂的社会语言学行为简化为了单纯的模式匹配。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我仔细读了你的论文，数据处理和模型构建的技术层面确实做得很扎实。但我的根本性质疑在于：你的框架将语码转换视为一种可以通过概率模型预测的语言行为，但这种处理方式忽略了语码转换最核心的维度——它是一种身份建构行为。一个人在微博上交替使用中英文，背后可能是阶层标识、文化认同协商、或是对特定受众的话语策略。你的模型能捕捉到'yyds'和'绝绝子'从英语借词演变为汉语网络用语的社会心理动力吗？你能解释为什么同一个说话者在不同语境下会选择完全不同的语码转换模式吗？纯粹的计算方法在面对这些问题时是否存在本质性的局限？
turns.text_pinyin: wǒ zǐxì dú le nǐ de lùnwén, shùjù chǔlǐ hé móxíng gòujiàn de jìshù céngmiàn quèshí zuò de hěn zhāshi. dàn wǒ de gēnběn xìng zhìyí zàiyú: nǐ de kuàngjià jiāng yǔmǎ zhuǎnhuàn shì wéi yī zhǒng kěyǐ tōngguò gàilǜ móxíng yùcè de yǔyán xíngwéi, dàn zhè zhǒng chǔlǐ fāngshì hūlüè le yǔmǎ zhuǎnhuàn zuì héxīn de wéidù — tā shì yī zhǒng shēnfèn jiàngòu xíngwéi. yī gè rén zài wēibó shàng jiāotì shǐyòng zhōng yīngwén, bèihòu kěnéng shì jiēcéng biāoshí, wénhuà rèntóng xiéshāng, huò shì duì tèdìng shòuzhòng de huàyǔ cèlüè. nǐ de móxíng néng bǔzhuō dào 'yyds' hé 'jué jué zǐ' cóng yīngyǔ jièlìcí yǎnbiàn wéi hànyǔ wǎngluò yòngyǔ de shèhuì xīnlǐ dònglì ma? nǐ néng jiěshì wèishéme tóng yī gè shuōhuà zhě zài bùtóng yǔjìng xià huì xuǎnzé wánquán bùtóng de yǔmǎ zhuǎnhuàn móshì ma? chúncuì de jìsuàn fāngfǎ zài miànduì zhèxiē wèntí shí shìfǒu cúnzài běnzhì xìng de júxiàn?
turns.text_en: I read your dissertation carefully — the technical aspects of data processing and model construction are indeed solid. But my fundamental challenge is this: your framework treats code-switching as a linguistic behavior predictable through probabilistic models, but this approach ignores code-switching's most core dimension — it's an identity construction behavior. When someone alternates between Chinese and English on Weibo, behind it may be class signaling, cultural identity negotiation, or discourse strategy targeting specific audiences. Can your model capture the sociopsychological dynamics of 'yyds' and 'juejuezi' evolving from English borrowings into Chinese internet slang? Can you explain why the same speaker would choose completely different code-switching patterns in different contexts? Does a purely computational approach face an inherent limitation when confronting these questions?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Defend your computational framework against the sociolinguistic critique.

options.text_zh: 谢谢陈教授的质疑，这也是我在论文写作过程中反复思考的核心张力。我的回应分为三个层次。首先，我需要澄清一个可能的误读：我的框架并非将语码转换简化为'纯粹的模式匹配'，而是提出了一种'计算社会语言学'的方法论——即通过大规模语料的量化分析，为社会语言学的质性洞察提供实证基础。这不是替代，而是互补。具体来说，我的模型的核心创新在于引入了'语境敏感的多层级分析框架'——它不仅追踪词汇层面的语码转换频率，更通过自然语言处理技术标注了每一次语码转换发生时的语用语境：发言者的社交网络位置、受众构成、话题类型和情感极性。正是这些语境变量的纳入，使得模型能够捕捉到您提到的'同一说话者在不同语境下的模式差异'。论文第五章的实验结果显示，当语境变量被纳入后，模型对语码转换位置的预测准确率从百分之六十七提升到了百分之八十三——这百分之十六的提升恰恰证明了社会语境因素的解释力，也说明我的模型已经在一定程度上整合了社会语言学的核心关切。其次，关于'yyds'的演变——这正是我论文第六章的案例研究。我追踪了这个表达在二〇二〇年到二〇二三年间的语用轨迹：从最初的英语借词式使用（仅出现在中英混合的语码转换语境中），到逐渐脱离英语语境成为独立的汉语网络词汇。我的模型通过分析其共现词汇网络的变化，量化了这一'去语码化'过程——当一个借词的共现网络从以英语词汇为主转变为以汉语词汇为主时，它就完成了从语码转换元素到汉语词汇的身份转变。这不正是用计算方法揭示了社会心理动力的运作轨迹吗？最后，我坦诚地承认计算方法确实存在局限——它无法直接捕捉个体层面的主观意图和身份认同。但我认为这种局限不是'本质性的'，而是'阶段性的'。随着多模态数据（语音、视频、社交关系图谱）的整合和解释性AI技术的发展，计算方法将越来越有能力接近社会语言学关心的那些深层问题。我的论文是朝这个方向迈出的一步，而非终点。
options.text_pinyin: xièxiè chén jiàoshòu de zhìyí, zhè yě shì wǒ zài lùnwén xiězuò guòchéng zhōng fǎnfù sīkǎo de héxīn zhānglì. wǒ de huíyìng fēnwéi sān gè céngcì. shǒuxiān, wǒ xūyào chéngqīng yī gè kěnéng de wùdú: wǒ de kuàngjià bìngfēi jiāng yǔmǎ zhuǎnhuàn jiǎnhuà wéi 'chúncuì de móshì pǐpèi', ér shì tíchū le yī zhǒng 'jìsuàn shèhuì yǔyán xué' de fāngfǎ lùn — jí tōngguò dà guīmó yǔliào de liànghuà fēnxī, wèi shèhuì yǔyán xué de zhìxìng dòngchá tígōng shízhèng jīchǔ. zhè bú shì tìdài, ér shì hùbǔ...
options.text_en: Thank you for the challenge, Professor Chen — this is also the core tension I repeatedly reflected on during the dissertation writing process. My response operates on three levels. First, I need to clarify a possible misreading: my framework doesn't reduce code-switching to 'pure pattern matching,' but proposes a 'computational sociolinguistics' methodology — using quantitative analysis of large-scale corpora to provide empirical foundations for sociolinguistic qualitative insights. This isn't replacement but complementarity. Specifically, my model's core innovation is introducing a 'context-sensitive multi-layered analysis framework' — it not only tracks lexical-level code-switching frequency but uses NLP techniques to annotate the pragmatic context of each code-switching instance: the speaker's social network position, audience composition, topic type, and sentiment polarity. It's precisely the inclusion of these contextual variables that enables the model to capture the 'pattern differences by the same speaker in different contexts' you mentioned. Chapter 5's experimental results show that when contextual variables are included, the model's prediction accuracy for code-switching position improved from 67% to 83% — this 16% improvement precisely demonstrates the explanatory power of social contextual factors and shows my model has already integrated the core concerns of sociolinguistics to a certain degree. Second, regarding 'yyds' evolution — this is precisely the case study in Chapter 6. I traced this expression's pragmatic trajectory from 2020 to 2023: from initial English-borrowing usage (appearing only in Chinese-English code-switching contexts) to gradually separating from English contexts to become an independent Chinese internet term. My model quantified this 'de-code-ification' process by analyzing changes in its co-occurrence lexical network — when a borrowing's co-occurrence network shifts from predominantly English vocabulary to predominantly Chinese vocabulary, it has completed its identity transformation from code-switching element to Chinese vocabulary item. Doesn't this demonstrate computational methods revealing the operational trajectory of sociopsychological dynamics? Finally, I candidly acknowledge that computational methods do have limitations — they cannot directly capture individual-level subjective intent and identity recognition. But I believe this limitation is 'phasic' rather than 'essential.' As multimodal data integration and interpretive AI technology develop, computational methods will increasingly approach the deep questions sociolinguistics cares about. My dissertation is a step in this direction, not the destination.
options.feedback: Academically rigorous — uses 计算社会语言学, 语境敏感的多层级分析框架, 语用语境, 共现词汇网络, 去语码化 with specialist precision. The three-level response (methodological clarification, empirical demonstration, honest limitation acknowledgment) is structurally impeccable for a dissertation defense. The 67% to 83% accuracy improvement directly addresses the critique with data. The 'yyds' case study elegantly demonstrates that computational methods can indeed capture social dynamics. Distinguishing 阶段性局限 from 本质性局限 shows philosophical sophistication about the nature of methodological boundaries.

options.text_zh: 陈教授的质疑很有道理。我承认计算方法确实不能完全解释社会动机，但它可以提供有价值的数据支持。我的论文主要贡献在技术层面，社会解释可以留给后续研究。
options.text_pinyin: chén jiàoshòu de zhìyí hěn yǒu dàolǐ. wǒ chéngrèn jìsuàn fāngfǎ quèshí bù néng wánquán jiěshì shèhuì dòngjī, dàn tā kěyǐ tígōng yǒu jiàzhí de shùjù zhīchí. wǒ de lùnwén zhǔyào gòngxiàn zài jìshù céngmiàn, shèhuì jiěshì kěyǐ liú gěi hòuxù yánjiū.
options.text_en: Professor Chen's challenge is well-founded. I admit computational methods can't fully explain social motivations, but they can provide valuable data support. My dissertation's main contribution is on the technical level — social interpretation can be left to subsequent research.
options.feedback: Concedes too much without defending the framework's sociolinguistic integration. In a dissertation defense, saying 'social interpretation can be left to subsequent research' when your committee member's core critique is precisely about social dimensions suggests the dissertation has a gap. Show how your model already incorporates social variables (语境变量, 受众构成, 社交网络位置) rather than deferring the question.

options.text_zh: 我认为计算方法和社会语言学方法不矛盾。两者各有优势，可以互相补充。我的论文侧重于计算分析，并不排斥社会语言学的解释。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ rènwéi jìsuàn fāngfǎ hé shèhuì yǔyán xué fāngfǎ bù máodùn. liǎngzhě gè yǒu yōushì, kěyǐ hùxiāng bǔchōng. wǒ de lùnwén cèzhòng yú jìsuàn fēnxī, bìng bù páichì shèhuì yǔyán xué de jiěshì.
options.text_en: I believe computational methods and sociolinguistic methods aren't contradictory. Both have their strengths and can complement each other. My dissertation focuses on computational analysis and doesn't exclude sociolinguistic interpretation.
options.feedback: Generic and non-responsive. The professor raised specific concerns about 身份建构, 阶层标识, and 文化认同协商 — responding with 'both can complement each other' without demonstrating how your model addresses these doesn't defend the dissertation. At a PhD defense, you must show the committee that your framework genuinely engages with their concerns, citing specific chapters, data, and results.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你提到的'去语码化'概念很有启发性，共现网络分析也确实是一个有创意的方法。但我还有最后一个问题——涉及研究伦理。你的语料库包含了超过五百万条微博数据。虽然这些是公开发布的内容，但当你对个体用户的语码转换模式进行深度分析时——尤其是你标注了他们的社交网络位置和受众构成——你是否在事实上构建了一种'语言画像'？在当前数据隐私意识高涨的背景下，这种研究是否存在伦理风险？你的论文中对此有充分的伦理反思吗？
turns.text_pinyin: nǐ tídào de 'qù yǔmǎ huà' gàiniàn hěn yǒu qǐfā xìng, gòngxiàn wǎngluò fēnxī yě quèshí shì yī gè yǒu chuàngyì de fāngfǎ. dàn wǒ hái yǒu zuìhòu yī gè wèntí — shèjí yánjiū lúnlǐ. nǐ de yǔliào kù bāohán le chāoguò wǔ bǎi wàn tiáo wēibó shùjù. suīrán zhèxiē shì gōngkāi fābù de nèiróng, dàn dāng nǐ duì gètǐ yònghù de yǔmǎ zhuǎnhuàn móshì jìnxíng shēndù fēnxī shí — yóuqí shì nǐ biāozhù le tāmen de shèjiāo wǎngluò wèizhì hé shòuzhòng gòuchéng — nǐ shìfǒu zài shìshí shàng gòujiàn le yī zhǒng 'yǔyán huàxiàng'? zài dāngqián shùjù yǐnsī yìshí gāozhǎng de bèijǐng xià, zhè zhǒng yánjiū shìfǒu cúnzài lúnlǐ fēngxiǎn? nǐ de lùnwén zhōng duì cǐ yǒu chōngfèn de lúnlǐ fǎnsī ma?
turns.text_en: The 'de-code-ification' concept you mentioned is quite inspiring, and the co-occurrence network analysis is indeed a creative method. But I have one final question — regarding research ethics. Your corpus contains over five million Weibo data points. Although these are publicly posted content, when you conduct deep analysis of individual users' code-switching patterns — especially since you've annotated their social network positions and audience compositions — are you in effect constructing a kind of 'linguistic profiling'? In the current context of heightened data privacy awareness, does this research carry ethical risks? Does your dissertation contain sufficient ethical reflection on this?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Address the research ethics concerns about linguistic profiling and data privacy.

options.text_zh: 这个问题问得非常及时，也正是当前计算语言学领域整体面临的伦理挑战。我在论文第二章的方法论部分专门用了一节来讨论研究伦理，但坦诚地说，陈教授的追问让我意识到这一节的深度可能还不够。让我说明我们实际采取的伦理保障措施，以及我对这个问题更深层的思考。在数据处理层面，我们采取了三重匿名化措施：第一，所有用户ID在数据采集阶段即被不可逆的哈希值替代；第二，任何可能间接识别个人身份的语境信息——具体地理位置、@提及、特定事件引用——都在预处理阶段被清除；第三，论文中呈现的所有分析结果都是聚合层面的统计模式，不涉及任何个体用户的可追溯案例。此外，我们的研究方案通过了北大社会科学研究伦理审查委员会的审批。但您提出的'语言画像'的概念确实指向了一个更深层的伦理问题——即便数据被匿名化了，大规模的语码转换模式分析本身是否可能被应用于群体层面的身份识别或歧视性画像？这是一个我在论文中没有充分展开、但确实需要正视的问题。我的立场是：计算社会语言学必须建立一套明确的'应用伦理边界'——研究成果可以用于学术理解和语言教育，但不应被用于商业化的用户画像或任何形式的社会监控。我计划在论文修订中加入一个专门的伦理章节，不仅讨论数据处理层面的合规性，更反思研究范式本身的社会责任。感谢陈教授推动我在这个方向上做更深入的思考。
options.text_pinyin: zhège wèntí wèn de fēicháng jíshí, yě zhèng shì dāngqián jìsuàn yǔyán xué lǐngyù zhěngtǐ miànlín de lúnlǐ tiǎozhàn...
options.text_en: This question is very timely and represents precisely the ethical challenge facing the computational linguistics field as a whole. I devoted a section in Chapter 2's methodology portion specifically to research ethics, but candidly, Professor Chen's follow-up makes me realize that section may lack sufficient depth. Let me explain the ethical safeguards we actually implemented, as well as my deeper thinking on this issue. At the data processing level, we implemented triple anonymization measures: first, all user IDs were replaced with irreversible hash values at the data collection stage; second, any contextual information that could indirectly identify individuals — specific geographic locations, @mentions, specific event references — was purged during preprocessing; third, all analytical results presented in the dissertation are aggregate-level statistical patterns with no traceable individual cases. Additionally, our research protocol was approved by Peking University's Social Science Research Ethics Review Committee. However, the 'linguistic profiling' concept you raise does point to a deeper ethical issue — even with anonymized data, could large-scale code-switching pattern analysis potentially be applied to group-level identity recognition or discriminatory profiling? This is a question I didn't sufficiently develop in the dissertation but genuinely needs to be confronted. My position is: computational sociolinguistics must establish a clear set of 'applied ethics boundaries' — research findings can be used for academic understanding and language education, but should not be used for commercialized user profiling or any form of social surveillance. I plan to add a dedicated ethics chapter in the dissertation revision, discussing not only data-processing compliance but reflecting on the social responsibility of the research paradigm itself. Thank you, Professor Chen, for pushing me toward deeper thinking in this direction.
options.feedback: Ethically mature — the triple anonymization framework (hash replacement, contextual purging, aggregate reporting) demonstrates rigorous data protection. Acknowledging the committee's approval while still accepting the critique shows intellectual honesty. The key move is distinguishing data-level compliance from paradigm-level responsibility — showing awareness that ethical research is not just about anonymization but about how findings might be applied. Thanking the professor for deepening your thinking is excellent dissertation defense etiquette — it signals growth rather than defensiveness.

options.text_zh: 我们的数据都是公开发布的微博内容，用户在发布时就已经放弃了隐私期待。我们也通过了学校的伦理审查。所有用户信息都做了匿名处理。
options.text_pinyin: wǒmen de shùjù dōu shì gōngkāi fābù de wēibó nèiróng, yònghù zài fābù shí jiù yǐjīng fàngqì le yǐnsī qīdài. wǒmen yě tōngguò le xuéxiào de lúnlǐ shěnchá. suǒyǒu yònghù xìnxī dōu zuò le nìmíng chǔlǐ.
options.text_en: Our data is all publicly posted Weibo content — users already gave up their privacy expectations when posting. We also passed the university's ethics review. All user information was anonymized.
options.feedback: Technically correct but ethically shallow. Claiming users 'gave up privacy expectations' by posting publicly is an increasingly contested position in data ethics. The professor raised a deeper question about 语言画像 and discriminatory applications — engage with that concern rather than retreating to procedural compliance. At the PhD level, demonstrate awareness of the broader ethical implications of your methodology.

options.text_zh: 这是一个很好的问题。我会在论文修改中加入更多关于伦理的讨论。
options.text_pinyin: zhè shì yī gè hěn hǎo de wèntí. wǒ huì zài lùnwén xiūgǎi zhōng jiārù gèng duō guānyú lúnlǐ de tǎolùn.
options.text_en: That's a great question. I'll add more discussion about ethics in my dissertation revision.
options.feedback: Acknowledges the gap but fails to demonstrate any ethical thinking in the moment. A dissertation defense requires you to engage substantively with questions, not promise future revisions. Describe your existing anonymization measures, acknowledge the deeper concern about 语言画像, and articulate a position on 应用伦理边界 — even if preliminary. The committee needs to see that you can think on your feet about ethical implications.

tree.cultural_note: PhD dissertation defenses at top Chinese universities are rigorous intellectual examinations where committee members probe the boundaries of the candidate's thinking. The format typically involves a 20-30 minute presentation followed by extended questioning from each committee member. Key terminology includes 答辩委员会 (defense committee), 独创性贡献 (original contribution), 方法论 (methodology), and 研究伦理 (research ethics). The most successful defenses demonstrate three qualities: technical mastery, intellectual honesty about limitations, and the ability to engage substantively with unexpected questions. Thanking committee members for 'pushing your thinking deeper' (推动我做更深入的思考) signals academic maturity and collegiality.
